etymology.description: semantic 水(水) (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic 先(先) (xiān) -> wash
etymology.interpretation: The character 洗(xǐ, "wash") combines the semantic component 水(水) (shuǐ, "water"), indicating the involvement of water in the action, with the phonetic component 先(先) (xiān), which provides the pronunciation. This pairing effectively conveys the meaning of washing or cleaning, which naturally involves water.

examples.chinese: 她每天洗衣服(她每天洗衣服)，所以总是很忙(所以總是很忙)。
examples.pinyin: Tā měitiān xǐ yīfú, suǒyǐ zǒngshì hěn máng.
examples.english: She washes clothes every day, so she is always busy.

examples.chinese: 他用热水洗脸(他用熱水洗臉)。
examples.english: He washes his face with hot water.
